article_html:
Sweet Couscous with Raisins and Almonds

By Published

This post may contain affiliate links.

10 minutes prep10 minutes cook6 servings
Hello, food enthusiasts! Today, we're diving into a delightful world of sweet couscous. This isn't your average savory side; we're transforming it into a delightful dessert. Sweet Couscous with Raisins and Almonds is a must-try!

Why I Love This Recipe

  1. 1
    I adore this recipe for its simplicity and unique blend of textures. It's a beautiful medley of chewy couscous, juicy raisins, and crunchy almonds. Plus, it's a refreshing change from typical desserts.

Ingredients

List of Required Ingredients

Let's dive right in! Here's what you'll need to create this magical dessert.

  • Couscous, Water, Raisins, Almonds, Sugar, Cinnamon, Salt.

That's it! Simple, everyday ingredients that combine to create something extraordinary.

Optional Ingredient Variations

Now, let's talk about variations. This recipe is incredibly versatile and caters to various dietary needs.

  • You can swap raisins for other dried fruits, use different nuts, or even play with spices to make it your own.

Don't be afraid to experiment and find your perfect blend!

Notes on Ingredient Quality

A quick note on ingredients: using good quality couscous and fresh spices will significantly enhance the flavor.

Step-by-Step Instructions

Prepping the Baking Pan

To begin, let's prep. You'll need a medium saucepan with a lid.

Mixing the Wet Ingredients

Start by boiling water. This will be used to cook our couscous.

Combining the Dry Ingredients

In a separate bowl, mix together the dry ingredients: couscous, sugar, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.

Folding in the White Chocolate and Cranberries

Once the couscous is cooked, fold in the raisins and almonds. Mix well to ensure even distribution.

Baking and Cooling Process

Now, let it rest. Fluff it gently with a fork, then let it cool. The couscous will absorb the flavors as it cools.

Tips & Tricks

How to Ensure Perfect Texture

Let's talk about texture. This dish is all about it!

  • To achieve the perfect texture, ensure you don't overcook the couscous. It should be light and fluffy, not mushy.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

Avoid these common mistakes for the best results:

  • Overcooking the couscous, Not fluffing the couscous after cooking, Using stale spices.

Suggestions for Serving and Pairing

Serving time! Here are some of my favorite ways to enjoy this sweet treat.

  • Serve it warm with a dollop of yogurt, Enjoy it at room temperature as a light snack, Or even chilled, straight from the fridge!

However you choose to serve it, it's sure to be a hit!

Pro Tips

1
Pro tip: Toasting the almonds before adding them to the couscous enhances their crunch and flavor.

Variations

Nutty White Chocolate Cranberry Blondies

For a nuttier flavor, you could add a mix of almonds and pistachios.

Gluten-Free Version

To make it gluten-free, simply use gluten-free certified couscous.

Alternative Flavor Combinations

Experiment with flavors by adding a drop of vanilla or almond extract.

Storage Info

Best Practices for Storing Blondies

Store Sweet Couscous with Raisins and Almonds in an airtight container.

How Long Do They Last?

It will keep well at room temperature for up to three days or in the fridge for up to a week.

Freezing Instructions

While you can freeze it, the texture might change slightly upon thawing.

FAQs

Can I use regular chocolate instead of white chocolate?
Can I add chocolate chips? Absolutely! Just fold them in like the raisins and almonds.
What can I substitute for cranberries?
Can I use dried cranberries instead of raisins? Yes! Any dried fruit will work beautifully.
How do I make blondies more chewy?
How do I keep the couscous chewy? Don't overcook it and fluff it immediately after cooking.
Can I double the recipe?
Can I double the recipe? Certainly! Just ensure your pan is large enough.

That's all, folks! Sweet Couscous with Raisins and Almonds is a delightful dessert that's sure to impress. Happy cooking!

Sweet Couscous with Raisins and Almonds

A delightful and aromatic twist on traditional couscous that combines the sweetness of raisins and the crunch of almonds.

⏱ 10 minutes prep
🍳 10 minutes cook
🍽 6 servings
⚡ 250 per serving cal

📝 Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups couscous
  • 1 1/2 cups water
  • 1/2 cup raisins
  • 1/2 cup almonds, sliced
  • 1/4 cup honey
  • 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
  • 1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 2 tablespoons butter
  • 1/4 cup orange juice
  • 1 teaspoon orange zest

📋 Instructions

  1. In a medium saucepan, bring the water to a boil. Add the couscous, remove from heat, and let it sit covered for 5 minutes.
  2. Fluff the couscous with a fork and stir in the raisins, almonds, honey, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, butter, orange juice, and orange zest.
  3. Cover the saucepan and let it sit for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
  4. Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with additional almonds if desired.

👨‍🍳 Chef's Notes

Course: Dessert
Cuisine: Mediterranean
